data.title: Lower Global Sugar Production Boosts Prices
data.description:
May NY world sugar #11 (SBK25) Thursday closed up +0.39 (+2.07%), and May London ICE white sugar #5 (SWK25) closed up +5.50 (+1.03%).

Sugar prices Thursday extended this week's advance, with NY sugar posting a 2-week high. Lower sugar production in Brazil supports prices after Unica reported Thursday that cumulative 2024/25 Center-South sugar output through February fell -5.6% y/y to 39.822 MMT. Sugar also has carryover support from Wednesday when the Indian Sugar and Bio-energy Manufactures Association cut its 2024/25 India sugar production forecast to 26.4 MMT from a January forecast of 27.27 MMT, citing lower cane yields.

Commodity Bulletin: From crude oil to coffee, this FREE newsletter is for industry pros and rookies alike

Meanwhile, the International Sugar Organization (ISO) last Thursday raised its 2024/25 global sugar deficit forecast to -4.88 MMT from a November forecast of -2.51 MMT, showing a tightening market from the 2023/24 global sugar surplus of 1.31 MMT. Last Thursday, the ISO also cut its 2024/25 global sugar production forecast to 175.5 MMT from a November forecast of 179.1 MMT. For its part, Green Pool Commodity Specialists on February 5 projected that the global sugar market will shift to a surplus of +2.7 MMT in the 2025/26 crop year from its estimate of a deficit of -3.7 MMT in 2024/25.

Last Thursday, sugar prices fell to 7-week lows due to signs of weak demand after sugar traders Wilmar International Ltd and Sucres et Denrees SA took record delivery of 1.7 MMT of raw sugar against the March NY futures contract that expired on February 28. Large deliveries are typically considered bearish for prices, indicating that sellers have few other markets to sell to.

Also on the bearish side for sugar was Wednesday's projection from consultant Datagro that 2025/26 Brazil Center-South sugar production of 42.4 MMT, up +6% y/y. In addition, sugar trader Czarnikow on February 27 projected Brazil's 2025/26 sugar production would climb to a record 43.6 MMT since producing sugar is more profitable than ethanol.

In a bearish factor, the Indian government said on January 20 that it would allow its sugar mills to export 1 MMT of sugar this season, easing the restrictions placed on sugar exports in 2023. India has restricted sugar exports since October 2023 to maintain adequate domestic supplies. India allowed mills to export only 6.1 MMT of sugar during the 2022/23 season to September 30 after allowing exports of a record 11.1 MMT in the previous season. However, the India Sugar Mills Association (ISMA) projects that India's 2024/25 sugar production will fall -17.5% y/y to a 5-year low of 26.4 MMT.

The outlook for higher sugar production in Thailand is bearish for sugar prices. On October 29, Thailand's Office of the Cane and Sugar Board projected that Thailand's 2024/25 sugar production would jump by +18% y/y to 10.35 MMT. Thailand produced 8.77 MMT of sugar in the 2023/24 season that ended in April. Thailand is the world's third-largest sugar producer and the second-largest sugar exporter.

Drought and excessive heat last year caused fires in Brazil that damaged sugar crops in Brazil's top sugar-producing state of Sao Paulo. Green Pool Commodity Specialists noted that as much as 5 MMT of sugar cane may have been lost due to the fires. Conab, Brazil's government crop forecasting agency, cut its 2024/25 Brazil sugar production estimate from November 21 to 44 MMT from a previous forecast of 46 MMT, citing lower sugarcane yields due to drought and excessive heat.

The USDA, in its bi-annual report released November 21, projected that global 2024/25 sugar production would climb +1.5% y/y to a record 186.619 MMT and that global 2024/25 human sugar consumption would increase +1.2% y/y to a record 179.63 MMT. The USDA also forecasted that 2024/25 global sugar ending stocks would decline -6.1% y/y to 45.427 MMT.

On the date of publication, Rich Asplund did not have (either directly or indirectly) positions in any of the securities mentioned in this article. All information and data in this article is solely for informational purposes. For more information please view the Barchart Disclosure Policyhere.

data.title: Greece Issues 3 Billion Euros in Dual-Tranche Syndicated Bond Tap — Update
data.description:
By Emese Bartha

Greece issued 3 billion euros ($3.27 billion) in a dual-tranche syndicated bond tap, involving July 2038- and June 2054-dated bonds on Thursday, one of the joint lead manager banks said.

Greece issued 2 billion euros in the 4.375% July 2038 bond, with this leg of the transaction attracting demand in excess of 35 billion euros, including 800 million euros in joint lead manager interest, one of the banks said. The spread on this bond was set at 126 basis points above mid-swaps.

The issue was priced at 103.206, at a yield of 4.057%, the same bank said.

In the other leg of the transaction, Greece issued 1 billion euros in the 4.125% June 2054 bond, for which books at reoffer were above 21.5 billion euros, including 300 million euros in joint lead manager interest, the same bank said.

The spread on this bond was set at 175 basis points above mid-swaps.

The issue was priced at 95.383, at a yield of 4.408%, the same bank said.

The outstanding volumes post issuance are 5.5 billion euros for the 2038-dated bond and 4 billion euros for the 2054-dated bond.

Joint lead managers of the transaction are Barclays, BNP Paribas, Commerzbank, Eurobank, J.P. Morgan and Piraeus Bank.

Write to Emese Bartha at emese.bartha@wsj.com

data.title: First Northwest Bancorp SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
First Northwest Bancorp, a bank holding company operating through its subsidiary First Fed Bank, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. First Northwest Bancorp focuses on community-oriented financial services and has made significant strides in both traditional banking and fintech investments.

Financial Highlights

First Northwest Bancorp reported the following key financial metrics for the year ending December 31, 2024:

  • Total Interest Income: $112.3 million, primarily due to an increase in the average balance of and higher yields on loans receivable.
  • Net Interest Income: $56.3 million, a decrease of 8.3% from the previous year due to additional interest expense related to higher costs on both deposit and advance balances.
  • Provision for Credit Losses: $16.5 million, a significant increase compared to the previous year due to loan balances charged-off and an increase in reserve for individually evaluated loans.
  • Net (Loss) Income: $(6.6) million, compared to a net income of $2.1 million in the previous year, primarily due to increased provision for credit losses and higher interest expenses.
  • Basic and Diluted (Loss) Earnings Per Common Share: $(0.75), compared to earnings of $0.26 per share in the previous year.

Business Highlights

First Northwest Bancorp's business operations in 2024 included the following highlights:

  • Business Overview: The company operates through its subsidiary, First Fed Bank, focusing on community-oriented financial services. It engages in both banking and non-banking financial activities, including strategic investments in fintech-related businesses.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates 18 locations across Washington State, including Clallam, Jefferson, King, Kitsap, Snohomish, and Whatcom counties. The market area is characterized by a mix of urban, suburban, and rural regions, with significant economic contributions from technology, services, and manufacturing industries.
  • Loan Portfolio Composition: As of December 31, 2024, the loan portfolio is diversified across real estate, consumer, and commercial business loans. Real estate loans, including one-to-four family, multi-family, and commercial real estate, constitute the majority of the portfolio.
  • Lending Activities: The bank focuses on real estate secured loans, including first lien one-to-four family mortgages, commercial, and multi-family loans. The bank also engages in construction and land loans, commercial business loans, and consumer loans, with a significant increase in commercial real estate and multi-family loan originations over the past five years.
  • Consumer Lending: The bank offers a range of consumer loans, including home equity loans, auto loans, and personal lines of credit. Auto loans are primarily purchased through partnerships with loan originators specializing in classic and collector vehicles.
  • Commercial Business Lending: The bank provides commercial business loans for general business purposes, secured by business assets. These loans are typically offered to small-to-medium sized companies within the bank's market area.
  • Investment Activities: The investment portfolio is managed to provide liquidity, generate earnings, and manage risk. The portfolio includes U.S. Treasury obligations, securities of federal agencies, municipal bonds, and mortgage-backed securities.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to extend its traditional and digital operations throughout the Puget Sound Region and beyond, leveraging its strategic investments in fintech to develop additional investment opportunities.

Strategic Initiatives

First Northwest Bancorp has undertaken several strategic initiatives to strengthen its market position and financial performance:

  • Loan Portfolio Repositioning: The company has focused on increasing the origination of commercial real estate, multi-family real estate, and construction and land loans.
  • Digital Infrastructure Expansion: The bank has expanded its digital infrastructure and enhanced its product offerings, including remote deposit capture and digital banking capabilities.
  • Fintech Investments: The company has invested in fintech companies through commitments to venture capital funds like Canapi Ventures, BankTech Ventures, and JAM FINTOP, aiming to gain early access to innovative technologies.
  • Capital Management: The company repurchased 312,288 shares of common stock during 2024, closing out the 2020 stock repurchase plan and initiating a new 2024 plan. It also paid $2.6 million in dividends. The bank completed a sale and leaseback of six branch properties, generating $14.7 million in proceeds used to pay down borrowings. Total shareholders' equity decreased by $9.5 million due to a net loss, share repurchases, and dividends paid. The bank maintained its 'well-capitalized' status under FDIC guidelines, with capital ratios exceeding regulatory requirements.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue expanding its market presence, particularly in Western Washington, and may consider acquiring additional branches or banks. The company aims to increase its focus on small-to-medium sized business relationships and explore alternative lending opportunities to improve interest income. It also intends to enhance its digital presence and streamline the customer experience, leveraging its investments in fintech to support growth in digital services.

Challenges and Risks

First Northwest Bancorp faces several challenges and risks that could impact its operations and financial performance:

  • Economic and Market Risks: Adverse economic conditions in Washington could impact earnings and increase credit risk. The company is also vulnerable to fluctuations in financial markets that may limit access to funding, affecting liquidity and capital levels.
  • Credit and Asset Quality Risks: Increased emphasis on commercial real estate lending and unsecured lending heightens the risk of loan losses. The rapid growth of the loan portfolio and expansion into new markets may increase credit risk due to less seasoned loans.
  • Operational Risks: The company faces competition for deposits, which may limit growth. Public health crises, geopolitical developments, and natural disasters could disrupt operations. Cybersecurity threats pose significant risks to the company's information systems.
  • Regulatory Risks: Changes in laws and regulations could adversely affect operations. The company's lending limit may restrict growth, and compliance with regulatory requirements is costly and complex.
  • Interest Rate Risks: The company is subject to interest rate risk, which could affect net interest income and the value of interest-earning assets. Changes in interest rates could also impact the ability to originate and sell loans.

Management acknowledges these challenges and plans to focus on strategic growth and improving operational efficiency to mitigate these risks. Efforts to enhance liquidity and manage interest rate risk are ongoing, with strategies to adjust the balance sheet as needed.

SEC Filing: First Northwest Bancorp [ FNWB ] - 10-K - Mar. 13, 2025

data.title: Canada Initiates Dispute Complaint Regarding US Steel and Aluminium Duties, says WTO
data.description:
Canada has requested dispute consultations with the United States regarding the imposition by the United States of import duties on certain steel and aluminium products from Canada, the World Trade Organization is confirming Thursday.

The request was circulated to WTO members on March 13, a statement says.

It noted Canada claims the measures, which terminate Canada's exemption from additional duties on certain steel and aluminium products and increase duties on aluminium articles, and which took effect on March 12, are inconsistent with US obligations under the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT) 1994.

data.title: Tariffs Unlikely to Result in Recession, Wells Fargo Says
data.description:
Escalating trade tensions have raised market uncertainty in recent weeks, but are unlikely to result in a recession or throw corporate profits off course, Wells Fargo Investment Institute said in a note emailed Thursday.

The advisory firm continues to expect gross domestic product to grow by 2.5% this year, which it said is adequate to keep corporate profits rising and the labor market strong. It projects another year of record earnings for companies in the S&P 500 index.

There will likely be a "temporary economic bump in the road that will not result in a recession," Wells Fargo Investment Institute Senior Global Market Strategist Scott Wren said.

Investor worries have mounted as President Donald Trump and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent have apparently discounted the market selloff and suggested that near-term volatility may be on the cards, according to the report.

Trump said Thursday that the US will impose a 200% tariff on all alcohol products coming from the EU if a European tariff placed on US whiskey is not rescinded. Canada and the EU recently announced retaliatory tariffs against the US.

"Uncertainty surrounding the ultimate outcome and effects of tariff policies should not be extrapolated to suggest the economy will ultimately deteriorate into a recession," Wren said.

Growth in the 2024 second half was strong so "some slowdown to a more sustainable pace makes sense," he said.

"The opportunity has arrived with the current pullback in the market that has taken the (S&P 500 index) 9.3% below the record high set just three ago," Wren said in the note that was dated Wednesday.

data.title: Purebread Brands Inc. Announces $5,577,570 Debt-to-Equity Conversion and 5-for-1 Share Consolidation
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - March 13, 2025) - Purebread Brands Inc. (TSXV: BRED) ("Purebread" or the "Company") is pleased to announce that it has entered into multiple shares-for-debt agreements with select lenders to convert $5,577,570 of outstanding debt (the "Debt Settlement") into an aggregate of 22,310,279 common shares of the Company ("Common Shares") at a price of $0.25 per Common Share, strengthening the Company's balance sheet and enhancing financial flexibility for future growth initiatives. Additionally, the Company will be implementing a 5-for-1 Common Share consolidation (the "Consolidation") to optimize its capital structure and enhance the long-term value for shareholders.

The Debt Settlement is being achieved at a pre-Consolidation price of $0.25 per Common Share, representing a premium to the current market price, allowing the Company to reduce its overall leverage and interest expenses. This strategic move aligns with the Company's commitment to improving shareholder value and positioning itself for long-term success.

After giving effect to the Consolidation, every five existing Common Shares will be consolidated into one new Common Share. The total number of outstanding Common Shares will be proportionally reduced, but each shareholder's percentage ownership in the Company and the value of their holdings will remain unchanged, subject to adjustments for fractional Common Shares. There are currently 115,830,277 Common Shares outstanding and it is anticipated that following the Consolidation, there will be approximately 23,166,055 Common Shares outstanding, prior to giving effect to the Debt Settlement. In accordance with the Articles of the Company, the Consolidation may be approved by the board of directors of the Company and shareholder approval is not required.

"By converting our financial obligations into equity at a significant premium to market and implementing a share consolidation, we are better positioned to pursue growth opportunities, enhance operational efficiencies, and improve marketability for our stock" said Amrit Maharaj, Interim CEO of Purebread Brands." Our team remains focused on delivering value to our shareholders and executing on our strategic plan."

After giving effect to the proposed Debt Settlement and Consolidation, Purebread will have significantly improved its capital structure, reducing its debt-to-equity ratio while ensuring a more efficient share structure. The Company continues to explore additional opportunities to optimize its financial strategy while driving sustainable growth.

Completion of the Debt Settlement and the Consolidation remain subject to receipt of all necessary regulatory approvals, including acceptance by the TSX Venture Exchange. The Company intends to effect the Consolidation following the closing of the Debt Settlement. All securities issued in connection with the Debt Settlement will be subject to a four-month hold period from the date of issuance in accordance with applicable Canadian securities laws, in addition to such other restrictions as may apply under applicable securities laws of jurisdictions outside Canada.

About Purebread Brands Inc.:

Purebread Brands Inc. is a leader in fast-casual cafe / bakeries in British Columbia, driving retail expansion in vibrant communities across Canada and beyond. Purebread is committed to crafting exceptional food experiences and making a positive impact on the communities it serves.

For more information and updated investor presentation, please visit www.purebreadbrands.com or contact:

Amrit Maharaj, Interim Chief Executive Officer

Purebread Brands Inc.

invest@purebread.ca

data.title: New York Dormitory Authority Plans $2 Billion Bond Sale for Public Works
data.description:
By Paulo Trevisani

The Dormitory Authority of the State of New York plans to issue $2.04 billion in state personal income tax revenue bonds to fund various public works and refund debt.

The general purpose bonds' proceeds will finance unspecified programs and projects across New York State, according to a preliminary statement published Wednesday on MuniOS.

The DASNY, created in 1944, provides financing services to non-profit institutions in healthcare, independent higher education and other areas. It issues debt on behalf of clients including the State University of New York, the City University of New York, the Department of Health and other agencies.

The planned offering includes $1.98 billion of tax-exempt Series 2025 A bonds and $56.97 million of federally taxable bonds, Series 2025 B, with yields to be determined.

The Series 2025 A bonds will mature between 2027 and 2055. The Series 2025B bonds will mature in 2026 and 2027.

The securities are expected to be delivered on or about March 27, according to the preliminary statement.

The bonds are payable from a statutory allocation of the state's revenues, including personal income, employer compensation expense and other receipts.

Write to Paulo Trevisani at paulo.trevisani@wsj.com

data.title: Gucci Names Balenciaga's Demna New Artistic Director in Bid to Revamp Brand — Update
data.description:
By Mauro Orru

French luxury-goods giant Kering appointed Demna Gvasalia as artistic director of Gucci, turning to the Balenciaga designer to inject new life into its flagship brand at a difficult time for the industry.

The group said Gvasalia, best known in the fashion world under his first name Demna, would take the helm of Gucci's creations in early July. His elevation from artistic director of Balenciaga, a role he has held since 2015, comes just over a month after Gucci said it was parting ways with designer Sabato De Sarno after he struggled to reinvigorate demand for the Italian brand.

"Demna's contribution to the industry, to Balenciaga, and to the Group's success has been tremendous," Kering Chief Executive Francois-Henri Pinault said in a statement. "His creative power is exactly what Gucci needs."

Gucci--which accounts for the lion's share of sales at Kering--has faced lackluster demand in recent months. Demna's appointment underscores efforts by Kering to reduce Gucci's reliance on younger, less affluent customers that are more susceptible to inflation and higher costs, in favor of higher-spending, aesthetically conservative buyers.

After years of strong results fueled by a surge in demand for luxury goods, the industry is grappling with slowing sales globally. China's economic woes added to the wider slowdown as clients in that key market cut back on spending. Kering booked revenue of 4.39 billion euros ($4.78 billion) for the last quarter of 2024, down 12% from the same quarter a year earlier. Gucci reported 1.92 billion euros in revenue, down 24% from the prior year.

De Sarno was meant to be a stabilizing force for the brand when Kering appointed him creative director in early 2023, but his designs failed to reignite demand. Demna's appointment marks the latest reshuffle at the conglomerate after Stefano Cantino's appointment last October as Gucci chief executive.

Write to Mauro Orru at mauro.orru@wsj.com

data.description: Front-month gold futures settled at a new record-high, closing up 1.5% to $2,984.30 a troy ounce. Today's close makes it the third consecutive session that front-month gold closed higher, gaining 3.2% in that time. The uptick brings gold closer to $3,000 an ounce level as major stock indexes enter or near correction territory. Tariff-related uncertainty is fueling a flight toward safety and the uptick in gold, says Robert Yawger of Mizuho Securities USA in a note. "Nowhere to hide but park cash in the shiny metal posting all-time highs," he says. SPDR Gold gains 1.6%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Blade Air Mobility, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Blade Air Mobility, Inc., a company specializing in air transportation and logistics services, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report highlights significant financial improvements, strategic initiatives, and operational achievements, alongside the challenges and risks the company faces in its industry. Blade's asset-light model and focus on transitioning to Electric Vertical Aircraft (EVA) are central to its growth strategy.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $248.7 million, increased by 10.4% from $225.2 million in 2023, driven by growth in the Medical segment and improved pricing in Passenger services.
  • Gross Profit: $40.7 million, increased by 81.0% from $22.5 million in 2023, reflecting a mix-shift to dedicated aircraft in the Medical segment and improved load factors in Passenger services.
  • Loss from Operations: $(33.9) million, improved from $(68.1) million in 2023, due to higher revenue and lower effective cost of revenue per flight.
  • Net Loss: $(27.3) million, improved by 51% from $(56.1) million in 2023, primarily due to increased revenue and cost efficiencies.
  • Net Loss Per Share: $(0.35), improved from $(0.76) in 2023, reflecting the overall improvement in net loss.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Blade Air Mobility, Inc. provides air transportation and logistics services, focusing on human organ transport for hospitals and passenger services with helicopters and fixed-wing aircraft, primarily in the Northeast United States and Southern Europe. The company operates an asset-light model, leveraging third-party aircraft and proprietary technology to transition to Electric Vertical Aircraft (EVA) for quieter, emission-free air mobility.
  • Revenue Segments: Blade operates in three key product lines: Short Distance (helicopter and seaplane flights), Jet and Other (non-medical jet charters and brand partnerships), and MediMobility Organ Transport (organ transportation and logistics services).
  • Geographical Performance: Blade's operations are concentrated in the Northeast United States and Southern Europe, with a focus on dense urban areas facing ground congestion. The company discontinued operations in Canada in August 2024.
  • Sales Units: In 2024, Blade reported 94,733 seats flown for all passenger flights, excluding discontinued Canadian operations. The company prioritizes by-the-seat sales to optimize flight profitability.
  • New Product Launches: Blade introduced the Trinity Organ Placement Services (TOPS) at the end of 2023, enhancing its MediMobility Organ Transport segment by offering donor logistics coordination and organ evaluation support.
  • New Production Launches: Over 2024, Blade acquired ten fixed-wing aircraft dedicated to the Medical segment, aiming to improve economies of scale and service reliability.
  • Future Outlook: Blade plans to leverage the lower operating costs of EVA to reduce consumer prices and expand vertiport infrastructure. The company anticipates EVA certification for public use, which will enable a seamless transition from conventional aircraft.
  • Technology and Innovation: Blade's proprietary technology stack supports real-time tracking, profit/loss analysis, and customer management, facilitating scalability and international expansion.
  • Competitive Landscape: Blade faces competition from Part 135 operators, brokers, and ground transportation services. The company differentiates itself through technology-enabled services, dedicated infrastructure, and a wide variety of aircraft types.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: Blade Air Mobility, Inc. has focused on expanding its asset-light business model by acquiring ten fixed-wing aircraft dedicated to the Medical segment, aiming to improve economies of scale and increase service reliability. The company has also invested in proprietary 'customer-to-cockpit' technology to manage operations efficiently and support future growth, including the transition to Electric Vertical Aircraft (EVA). Additionally, Blade has expanded its geographic presence by acquiring entities in Southern Europe, enhancing its market reach in Southern France, Monaco, Italy, and Switzerland.
  • Capital Management: Blade has maintained a strong liquidity position with $127.1 million in total liquid funds as of December 31, 2024, consisting of cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments. The company has implemented a stock repurchase program authorized to buy back up to $20 million of its common stock, with $0.2 million repurchased in 2024. Blade has also managed its capital expenditures by investing $30.9 million in property and equipment, primarily for aircraft acquisitions. The company has no intention of declaring or paying cash dividends in the foreseeable future.
  • Future Outlook: Blade plans to continue leveraging its asset-light model and proprietary technology to facilitate the transition to EVA, aiming to reduce operating costs and expand its service offerings. The company anticipates no need for additional capital to execute its current business plan over the next 12 months, focusing on strategic expansion into new markets and enhancing its existing routes. Blade is also committed to maintaining a flexible cost structure through capacity purchase agreements and optimizing its owned and third-party aircraft utilization.

Challenges and Risks

  • Operational Risks: The company faces significant operational risks due to its reliance on third-party aircraft operators. Any failure by these operators to perform adequately could lead to increased costs and disruptions in service. Additionally, the company's expansion into international markets, such as Europe and India, presents challenges related to integration, regulatory compliance, and competition from local incumbents.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks associated with the adoption of Electric Vertical Aircraft (EVA) technology. The success of this technology is uncertain, as it depends on regulatory approvals, market acceptance, and the ability of third-party operators to integrate EVA into their operations. Any delays or failures in these areas could adversely affect the company's growth prospects.
  • Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks are heightened by the heavily regulated nature of the healthcare and aviation industries. Changes in healthcare policies, particularly those affecting organ procurement and transportation, could impact the company's Medical segment. Additionally, the company must navigate complex aviation regulations, which could affect its ability to expand its route network and adopt new technologies like EVA.
  • Emerging Risks: Emerging risks include the potential impact of climate change on operations, as severe weather events could disrupt flights and damage infrastructure. The company is also vulnerable to geopolitical conflicts and terrorist attacks, which could deter passengers from flying and increase security costs.
  • Management's Discussion and Analysis: Management has identified the need for substantial investments in infrastructure, technology, and marketing to support growth. The company plans to enhance its operational systems and internal controls to manage expansion effectively. However, these efforts require significant capital and may not yield the expected returns if market conditions or consumer preferences shift.
  • Market Risk Disclosures: The company is exposed to fluctuations in aircraft fuel prices, which could impact operating costs and profitability. Additionally, foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations pose a risk to the company's international operations, potentially affecting financial results.

SEC Filing: Blade Air Mobility, Inc. [ BLDE ] - 10-K - Mar. 13, 2025

data.title: American University in Cairo Invites Silicon Valley Entrepreneur Safeer Qureshi for TEDx Talk
data.description:
Silicon Valley, California--(Newsfile Corp. - March 13, 2025) - The American University in Cairo (AUC) is set to host TEDxEtTagammo on April 26, 2025, bringing together thought leaders, innovators, and industry experts to explore how ideas create lasting impact. With the theme "Echoes", the event will highlight the ways in which decisions, innovations, and leadership continue to shape the world long after their inception.

Among the speakers at this year's event is Safeer Qureshi, a Silicon Valley based, Canadian entrepreneur and business strategist known for his work with global enterprises, key organizations, and influential leaders. His talk will explore how ideas, innovation, and strategic thinking can drive meaningful change-reflecting the event's theme of how actions and decisions continue to shape the world long after they are made.

TEDxEtTagammo has become a platform for engaging discussions on the future of business, technology, and leadership. With more than a decade of experience building, scaling, and advising businesses across multiple industries, Safeer Qureshi has worked with corporations, multinational organizations, and international governments to navigate an evolving technological landscape. His insights into innovation and transformative business strategies align with the event's exploration of how ideas continue to resonate across industries and societies for ages.

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/11530/244448_785dc5a6a40c087d_001full.jpg

"Ideas don't just exist in the moment-they spark change that can shape the future in ways we may never fully see," said Qureshi. "I've had the privilege of working alongside leaders and organizations driving real change. At TEDxEtTagammo, I'm going to share how they do it. How the right ideas, combined with action, can leave a lasting mark on industries, economies, and beyond."

The event will bring together speakers from various backgrounds, each sharing insights on how ideas and actions can create lasting change. From advancements in technology to shifts in economic and social structures, TEDxEtTagammo aims to highlight the unintended and far-reaching consequences of bold ideas.

Date: April 26, 2025

Location: The American University in Cairo, New Cairo campus

Theme: "Echoes"
